title: banksia rufa subsp. pumila
intro: Banksia rufa subsp. pumila, also known as the Dwarf Red Banksia, is a small to medium-sized shrub native to the southwestern regions of Western Australia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The Dwarf Red Banksia is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory problems, and providing antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are typically used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
